title_guess: Ross-on-Wye, Symonds Yat & Tintern Abbey (with John)

pages.text:
Friday April 24th
   We left at 5.30 + reached The White House at Ross at 6.45. It's on the side of the hill overlooking the Wye. In the 1700s a benefactor called Kyrle did alot of good works beautifying the town. The B+B owner spent ages mentioning every restaurant in Ross + whether they [?are good] [illegible]

We walked from this view-point Yat Rock down to river + up again.

hrs.

They wd. take dogs. Tinder was allowed in the bedroom & given a sausage for breakfast. (John is embarrassed that we have called the dog Tinder because it is a sex website. People laugh when they hear us calling the dog.) Supper in van

__Saturday__
Simon & I spent ages in the second hand book shop. John arrived at 11.15  We had coffee in the van wandered in town & lunch in the van. Then we went in John's whizzy turquoise car to Symonds Yat. Fantastic views. Strenuous walk. Supper at B+B owner (Judy's) brother's Bistro in town. Good Portugese cook. Tagine. B+B v. comfortable but carpets grubby. John drove to Symonds Yat, Simon drove back. V. powerful, noisy engine.

__Sunday April 26__[th]
Another huge breakfast & on to Tintern Abbey. Gorgeous ride thro spring green colours. V. cold. Peaceful Abbey, well worth a visit. After coffee John set off back to the Mumbles & Simon & Eve & Tinder had lunch in the van at Westbury Court Garden.

captions: Cistercian Monks, 11[th]B. Liked isolated places. R. Wye
pages.text:
Cistercian Monks, 11[th]B. Liked isolated places. R. Wye

We drove over hills from Wye to Severn.

captions: Enjoy Westbury Court Garden — Rediscover a formal Dutch water garden (National Trust brochure cover, glued in)
pages.text:
